name: Ubuntu Desktop (GPU)
description: Ubuntu 26.04 KDE Plasma (X11) desktop in a privileged LXC container, displayed on the Proxmox host's physical console with GPU passthrough. Uses LightDM (GDM/SDDM freeze in LXC), runs the container's own udevd for console input hotplug, and ships a DRM-hotplug handler so the login screen appears when a monitor is connected after a headless boot.

notes.text: A desktop user (default: 'desktop') and a generated password are saved to /root/ubuntu-desktop-gpu.creds inside the container. Override the user/locale/keymap with the CTUSER, CTLOCALE and CTKEYMAP environment variables at launch.
Requires a privileged container with AppArmor unconfined and /sys mounted rw so the container's udevd can handle console input hotplug. This lowers isolation and is not a security boundary.

Connect a monitor/keyboard to the Proxmox host (e.g. via KVM) to use the desktop on the physical console. Tested with Intel Arc (Battlemage); AMD should work via the generic modesetting driver; NVIDIA additionally needs its userspace driver installed in the container.
